title: Artemis II Exits Lunar Sphere of Influence, Crew Sets Distance Record en Route Home
detail: On April 7, the Artemis II Orion spacecraft exited the lunar sphere of influence at approximately 1:25 p.m. ET, at 41,072 miles from the Moon, and began its return trajectory toward Earth. The crew — Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch (NASA), and Jeremy Hansen (CSA) — set a new crewed distance record of 252,760 miles from Earth, surpassing Apollo 13's previous record. During the lunar flyby on April 6, the crew also observed a total solar eclipse from space, with the Sun blocked by the Moon for approximately 53 minutes. President Trump phoned the crew, telling them 'You've made history.' Splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off San Diego is targeted for approximately April 11.

media.caption: Artemis II crew exits lunar sphere of influence, sets crewed distance record at 252,760 miles from Earth

title: Trump Administration Invoking 2024 SCOTUS Immunity Ruling to Claim Unreviewable Executive Authority
detail: An ABC News review published April 7 found that Trump and his legal team had cited the Supreme Court's 2024 Trump v. United States presidential immunity ruling in nearly one-third of 29 emergency applications to the Supreme Court during his second term. The invocations span claims of unreviewable authority over federal firings, immigration enforcement, trade decisions, and military deployments. Legal scholars noted the administration was interpreting the ruling far more broadly than its original scope, which addressed criminal prosecution of a former president, potentially setting precedents with long-term constitutional implications.

media.caption: Trump Administration Invoking 2024 SCOTUS Immunity Ruling to Claim Unreviewable Executive Authority

title: Markets Continue Sharp Selloff; Recession Warnings Intensify Amid Tariff Escalation
detail: US equity markets opened lower on April 7 as multi-day tariff-driven volatility continued, with the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq each down roughly 9–11% from recent highs following the Liberation Day anniversary tariff escalations. Analysts drew comparisons to the 1987 Black Monday selloff. JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon issued a shareholder letter warning of rising recession probability. The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model flashed a negative reading for Q1 2026, raising stagflation concerns. Trump's social media messaging framing the selloff as intentional added to investor uncertainty, while the administration maintained that tariffs were necessary to correct longstanding trade imbalances.

title: US Marshals Deputized Musk's Private Security as Federal Agents — NBC Investigation
detail: NBC News reported on April 6–7 that US Marshals Service waived standard firearms training requirements to deputize members of Elon Musk's private security team as federal agents in February 2025, granting them authority to carry weapons in federal buildings during DOGE's government access phase. The revelation came weeks after Musk announced he would scale back DOGE involvement. Critics raised concerns about private security personnel receiving federal law enforcement powers with reduced vetting, and questioned oversight mechanisms for the arrangement.

media.caption: US Marshals Deputized Musk's Private Security as Federal Agents — NBC Investigation
